purpose: Resolver-only test after web-search, adjudication, line-geometry endpoint, and conservative review-policy updates. Expected points are approximate km-scale review anchors.

cases.center_description: entrance of Marina del Rey Harbor where it meets Santa Monica Bay
cases.label: Marina del Rey Harbor entrance opening to Santa Monica Bay
cases.reason: The requested target is the practical seaward entrance of Marina del Rey Harbor where the main channel opens into Santa Monica Bay. Evidence consistently describes this as the entrance channel between the north and south jetties / at the breakwater, not the inland harbor centroid. A 2 km DEM centered near the midpoint of that jetty opening will straddle both the harbor mouth and Santa Monica Bay, which matches the workflow intent.

evidence.title: Marina del Rey Harbor study appendix with station MdRH-MC3
evidence.summary: Lists station 'MdRH-MC3' as 'Main Channel, entrance' at 33°58.880' N, 118°27.316' W, directly supporting the entrance location.

evidence.title: 33 CFR 162.200 restricted area description
evidence.summary: Describes the relevant waters as the most seaward 1,000 feet of the entrance channel between the north and south jetties, confirming the functional entrance target.

evidence.title: USACE Marina del Rey navigation page
evidence.summary: Refers to dredging and navigation safety in the Marina del Rey entrance and approach channels, reinforcing that the entrance channel is the practical coastal connection to the bay.

evidence.title: LA County navigation and regulations page
evidence.summary: Provides local harbor navigation context and mentions entrance conditions, supporting that the harbor mouth is an actively managed navigational opening.

rejected_candidates: Marina del Rey community/place centroid: rejected because the request is for the harbor entrance where it meets the bay, not the broader district.
Harbor interior or main basin centroid: rejected because it is inland of the practical ocean connection.

Far offshore end of the approach channel near or beyond the detached breakwater: rejected because the DEM target should be the shoreline/breakwater opening, not a farther offshore navigation endpoint.
North or south jetty access points/parking areas: rejected because they are land access features, not the water opening itself.
cases.review_reason: Web resolver agreed only with an ungrounded text fallback; inspect the context maps before using this AOI.

cases.center_description: harbor entrance where Santa Cruz Harbor opens to Monterey Bay
cases.label: Santa Cruz Harbor entrance opening between the east and west jetties, where the harbor opens to Monterey Bay
cases.reason: The user wants the physical harbor entrance where Santa Cruz Harbor opens to Monterey Bay. The web candidate explicitly targets the opening between the jetties, which is the correct practical DEM center for a 2 km coastal box. The grounded candidate is about 1.7 km away and appears too far south, inconsistent with Santa Cruz Harbor’s actual entrance area. For this request, the seaward jetty-gap midpoint is preferred over any generic harbor or inferred point.

evidence.title: Santa Cruz Port District - Location
evidence.summary: Official harbor source gives Walton Lighthouse at the end of the breakwater at 36°57.38′N, 122°00.05′W, placing the west jetty tip at the harbor entrance.

evidence.title: Santa Cruz Port District - West Jetty Repair Project notice
evidence.summary: Official source states the East and West Jetties were constructed to maintain a stabilized entrance channel, confirming the practical target is the opening between them.

evidence.title: US Harbors - Santa Cruz (Monterey Bay), CA
evidence.summary: Describes shoaling at the harbor entrance, reinforcing that the navigable mouth/opening is the coastal feature of interest, not the interior harbor.

rejected_candidates: Santa Cruz Harbor / Small Craft Harbor centroid — rejected because the request is specifically for the entrance where it opens to Monterey Bay.
Walton Lighthouse point itself — rejected as a structure on the west breakwater, not the center of the entrance opening.
Santa Cruz city centroid — rejected because it is administrative and not the coastal hydraulic target.
Far offshore navigation alignment/channel endpoint — rejected because the DEM center should be at the shoreline/breakwater opening, not offshore.
cases.review_reason: Resolver candidates disagreed beyond the domain-scale threshold; LLM adjudicated the center, but analyst map review is required.

cases.center_description: entrance of Oceanside Harbor where the harbor meets the ocean
cases.label: Oceanside Harbor ocean entrance opening between the west breakwater and east/south jetty, Oceanside, California
cases.reason: The request is specifically for the harbor entrance where Oceanside Harbor meets the ocean. Authoritative marine sources describe a single common entrance between two jetties, with the breakwater and south jetty forming the entrance channel. For a 2 km DEM, the practical center should be placed at the seaward jetty opening/channel mouth so the box spans both the harbor side and the Pacific, not the inland harbor basin or a generic harbor centroid.

evidence.title: USACE Los Angeles District – Oceanside navigation project
evidence.summary: States that the harbor's breakwater and south jetty form an entrance channel, directly matching the requested harbor mouth feature.

evidence.title: 33 CFR 80.1108 – Oceanside Harbor, CA
evidence.summary: Defines the harbor entrance line from Oceanside South Jetty Light 4 to Oceanside Breakwater Light 3, confirming the practical entrance is the opening between those structures.

evidence.title: MarineLink Ports Directory – Oceanside
evidence.summary: Describes Oceanside as having a single main entrance with two protective structures, supporting a single harbor-mouth target rather than multiple candidate mouths.

evidence.title: NOAA Tides & Currents station 9410396 – Oceanside Harbor
evidence.summary: Confirms the harbor location and supports the general placement at the harbor mouth area rather than an inland basin or city centroid.

rejected_candidates: Oceanside city centroid — administrative place, not the harbor entrance.
Oceanside Harbor office / Harbor Drive facilities — inland operational site, not where the harbor meets the ocean.
Harbor interior basins or fishing pier — inside the harbor, not the mouth opening.
Far offshore entrance-channel endpoint — not the practical shoreline/breakwater opening requested.
cases.review_reason: Web resolver agreed only with an ungrounded text fallback; inspect the context maps before using this AOI.

cases.center_description: harbor entrance channel between Morro Rock and the breakwater
cases.label: Morro Bay Harbor entrance channel opening between Morro Rock and the west breakwater
cases.reason: The user's target is the practical harbor entrance between Morro Rock and the breakwater, so the DEM center should be placed at the seaward opening/channel throat rather than at Morro Rock, the town/harbor centroid, or an inland harbor basin. NOAA Coast Pilot describes Morro Rock on the north side of the entrance, a breakwater extending south from the rock, and the entrance channel between protective breakwaters. Multiple sources give entrance coordinates clustered near 35.36 N, -120.87 W, which match the requested opening well enough for a 2 km DEM centered on the harbor mouth.

evidence.title: USACE Morro Bay navigation history
evidence.summary: States access from the north side of Morro Rock was eliminated when the seawall/causeway connected Morro Rock to the mainland, implying the functional entrance is the south-side opening.

evidence.title: Historical/official harbor description
evidence.summary: Explains that before the causeway the harbor entrance was from the north, and later USACE built the outer breakwater to protect the new entrance to the harbor, confirming the present entrance is the protected opening south of Morro Rock.

evidence.title: Local description of entrance location
evidence.summary: Describes the entrance to the bay as lying between Morro Rock and the breakwater jetties, matching the user's target phrasing.

evidence.title: USACE/EA entrance reference
evidence.summary: Gives approximate entrance coordinates for Morro Bay Harbor near 35°21'4"N, 120°52'07"W, supporting a center in the entrance reach; the chosen point is adjusted slightly seaward/northwest to the middle of the practical opening between the rock and breakwater.

rejected_candidates: Morro Bay city centroid rejected because the request is for the harbor entrance opening, not the municipality.
Morro Rock centroid/base rejected because the target is the channel gap adjacent to the rock, not the rock itself.
Harbor interior/marina area rejected because it is inland of the entrance opening.
Far offshore entrance-channel endpoint rejected because the workflow wants the practical shoreline/breakwater opening where the harbor meets the ocean.
North side of Morro Rock rejected because official/history sources indicate the former north entrance was closed by the causeway/seawall.
cases.review_reason: Web resolver agreed only with an ungrounded text fallback; inspect the context maps before using this AOI.

cases.center_description: Mission Bay entrance channel where Mission Bay opens to the Pacific Ocean
cases.label: Mission Bay entrance channel opening between the north and south jetties, San Diego, California
cases.reason: The user's intent is the practical ocean connection where Mission Bay opens to the Pacific. The web candidate explicitly targets the jetty-gap midpoint/seaward opening, which is the preferred DEM center for an entrance-channel request and would straddle both the bay channel and open ocean. The grounded candidate is substantially west-southwest of that and appears too far offshore/alongshore relative to the physical inlet opening. Therefore the web candidate best represents the requested shoreline connection.

evidence.title: USACE San Diego–Mission Bay fact sheet
evidence.summary: Authoritative source identifying Mission Bay's entrance channel and jetties as the engineered inlet system.

evidence.title: San Diego Kayak Club: Mission Bay Entrance
evidence.summary: Describes the mouth/entrance as the full-width opening between the north and south jetties and discusses navigation through that gap.

evidence.title: Historic USACE environmental statement for Mission Bay entrance channel dredging
evidence.summary: Confirms the entrance channel and jetties were constructed/dredged to form the ocean access opening at Mission Bay.

rejected_candidates: Mission Bay city/place centroid — too broad and not the requested coastal opening.
Mission Bay Channel topo feature centroid — may represent a channel label rather than the shoreline opening.
Jetty parking/access points on either shore — land access locations, not the hydraulic entrance center.
Far offshore navigation alignment endpoint — not the practical DEM center for 'where Mission Bay opens to the Pacific Ocean'.
cases.review_reason: Resolver candidates disagreed beyond the domain-scale threshold; LLM adjudicated the center, but analyst map review is required.

cases.center_description: entrance of Newport Harbor between the jetties at the ocean
cases.label: Newport Harbor ocean entrance midpoint between the west and east jetties, Newport Beach, California
cases.reason: The user's target is the physical ocean entrance of Newport Harbor between the jetties. The grounded candidate at -117.9329, 33.5962 is clearly wrong for Newport Harbor because it is about 3.1 km west of the actual harbor mouth and does not plausibly fall at the Newport Bay/Newport Harbor entrance. The web candidate matches the requested feature type and explicitly identifies the midpoint of the seaward jetty opening, which is the correct practical DEM center for a 2 km box straddling harbor and Pacific Ocean. Confidence is medium rather than high because the two supplied candidates conflict strongly and only the web candidate is plausibly located at the harbor entrance.

evidence.title: City of Newport Beach – Visiting Vessels
evidence.summary: Official harbor guidance says the broad entrance channel is formed by two jetties, confirming the practical target is the opening between them.

evidence.title: NOAA Tides & Currents – Newport Beach, Newport Bay Entrance (Station 9410580)
evidence.summary: NOAA maintains a station specifically named for the Newport Bay entrance, anchoring the entrance location.

evidence.title: NOAA benchmark sheet for station 9410580
evidence.summary: Lists the entrance station latitude as 33.60330, supporting a center near the harbor mouth.

evidence.title: NOAA Coast Pilot 7 / nautical references
evidence.summary: Describes the entrance to Newport Bay as being between jetties 275 yards apart, reinforcing that the correct DEM target is the jetty gap itself.

rejected_candidates: Newport Beach city centroid — administrative place, not the requested coastal opening.
Newport Harbor interior centroid — too far inside the bay; not the ocean entrance.
West Jetty or East Jetty tip alone — user asked for the entrance between the jetties, so a midpoint is more appropriate.
Entrance buoy / offshore channel marker — offshore navigation aid, not the practical shoreline/breakwater opening.
Upper Newport Bay / Back Bay mouth — different internal connection, not where the harbor meets the ocean.
cases.review_reason: The user's target is the physical ocean entrance of Newport Harbor between the jetties. The grounded candidate at -117.9329, 33.5962 is clearly wrong for Newport Harbor because it is about 3.1 km west of the actual harbor mouth and does not plausibly fall at the Newport Bay/Newport Harbor entrance. The web candidate matches the requested feature type and explicitly identifies the midpoint of the seaward jetty opening, which is the correct practical DEM center for a 2 km box straddling harbor and Pacific Ocean. Confidence is medium rather than high because the two supplied candidates conflict strongly and only the web candidate is plausibly located at the harbor entrance.

cases.reason: The user's target is the practical harbor entrance where Brookings Harbor opens to the Pacific, not the marina basin or a generic Brookings centroid. evidence.title: Oregon State Parks / Yaquina Bay State Recreation Site

cases.label: Coos Bay entrance between the north and south jetties

cases.center_description: mouth of New River Inlet where it enters the Atlantic Ocean
cases.label: New River Inlet ocean entrance midpoint, between Onslow Beach spit and North Topsail Beach ocean shoreline
cases.reason: The user asked for the mouth of New River Inlet where it enters the Atlantic Ocean. The practical DEM center should be the seaward opening itself, not the named-feature centroid or an inland channel point. Recent USACE hydrographic imagery shows the active inlet throat opening to the Atlantic between the Onslow Beach spit on the north/west side and North Topsail Beach on the south side. A 2 km × 2 km DEM centered near the midpoint of that opening will straddle both the inlet throat and adjacent Atlantic surf zone.

evidence.title: Natural Atlas: New River Inlet
evidence.summary: Identifies New River Inlet as an inlet on the Atlantic Ocean near Sneads Ferry, confirming the feature type and general location.

evidence.title: TopoZone: New River Inlet
evidence.summary: Lists New River Inlet at approximately 34.5273875, -77.3369029, giving a direct named-feature coordinate close to the ocean opening.

evidence.title: TopoZone: New River
evidence.summary: Lists nearby New River coordinates around 34.5298875, -77.3371807, consistent with the river/inlet mouth area and supporting a center in the throat of the opening.

evidence.title: US Harbors: New River Inlet, NC
evidence.summary: Describes navigation above the mouth, implying the functional entrance is the inlet opening rather than an inland point.

rejected_candidates: Sneads Ferry town centroid — administrative/community location, not the ocean entrance.
NDBC Station 41109 'New River Inlet' — offshore buoy/station, not the shoreline inlet mouth.
Generic inlet feature centroid if placed inland or broad-waterbody centered — less precise than the practical opening requested.
Far offshore navigation-channel approach endpoint — user asked for where the inlet enters the Atlantic, so the shoreline opening/throat is the correct DEM center.

cases.center_description: shoreline at the USACE Field Research Facility research pier in Duck
cases.label: Shoreline at the landward base of the USACE Field Research Facility research pier, Duck, North Carolina
cases.reason: The user asked for the shoreline at the USACE Field Research Facility research pier in Duck, not the town center or pier end. Multiple USACE sources confirm the FRF is the well-known Duck research pier on the Atlantic Ocean in Duck, NC, at 1261 Duck Road, with a 560 m (1,840 ft) pier extending seaward from the dune/shoreline. For a 2 km × 2 km DEM, the practical center is the beach/shoreline where the pier meets land so the domain straddles the surf zone and adjacent back-beach/pier area rather than centering offshore at the pier head.

cases.center_description: ocean entrance of Government Cut by South Beach where it meets the Atlantic
cases.label: Government Cut ocean entrance midpoint between the seaward ends of the north and south jetties, off South Pointe/Fisher Island
cases.reason: The user asked for the ocean entrance of Government Cut where it meets the Atlantic by South Beach. For a coastal DEM, the practical target is not an inland channel point or harbor centroid, but the jettied Atlantic opening itself. Evidence consistently describes Government Cut as the jettied ocean entry to Miami Harbor at South Beach, between Miami Beach/South Pointe and Fisher Island. A 2 km × 2 km DEM centered near the midpoint of that seaward gap will straddle both the outer Atlantic and the inlet throat.

evidence.title: Marinas.com inlet description
evidence.summary: Calls Government Cut the main ocean entry into the Miami area at South Beach and notes Miami Beach Marina lies just inside the inlet on the north side, confirming the functional inlet mouth.

evidence.title: USACE / port references
evidence.summary: States the ocean entrance channel to Miami Harbor passes through Government Cut, a man-made inlet at the southern end of Miami Beach; supporting inlet-mouth interpretation rather than an inland harbor centroid.

evidence.title: South Pointe / jetty context
evidence.summary: Places the South Pointe jetty directly north of Government Cut inlet, helping locate the practical opening at the jettied entrance.

rejected_candidates: South Pointe Park centroid — park/viewpoint, not the water entrance itself.
PortMiami / Dodge Island centroid — inland port area, not where Government Cut meets the Atlantic.
Miami Beach city centroid — administrative/place centroid, not a coastal entrance target.
Far offshore Miami Harbor Entrance navigation reference — too seaward for the practical shoreline/breakwater opening requested.

cases.center_description: Clearwater Pass inlet where it meets the Gulf of Mexico
cases.label: Clearwater Pass seaward inlet opening between the north and south jetties, Clearwater Beach/Sand Key, Florida
cases.reason: The user's intent is explicitly the inlet mouth where Clearwater Pass meets the Gulf of Mexico. For a coastal DEM, that practical target is the seaward opening between the jetties so the 2 km box straddles both the pass and open Gulf water. The web candidate matches that physical shoreline/opening interpretation and is supported by inlet-jetty evidence. The grounded candidate is about 1.75 km farther north, which would shift the DEM away from the actual seaward pass opening and does not fit the requested mouth location as well.

evidence.title: USACE Jacksonville District – Clearwater Pass FL (O&M)

Sand Key Bridge / Clearwater Pass Bridge — bridge crossing over the pass, but not the Gulf-facing entrance itself.
Clearwater Harbor or ICW interior channel — inland waterbody/channel segment, not where the inlet meets the Gulf.
Far offshore dredged/navigation channel endpoint in the Gulf — too far seaward for the requested inlet mouth target.
